items.title: For decades Australian house prices have grown. This is what a fall could mean

children.content: After decades of growth Australian house prices are slowing. Here's what a fall could look like.

items.title: Albanese wants conference to 'showcase' Labor but not its AUKUS division

children.content: The prime minister declares the conference would be "a real opportunity to bring the whole Labor movement with us on our direction".

items.title: MP calls for investigation into anti-abortion campaigner

children.content: Anti-abortion campaigner Joanna Howe has been called out for potentially breaching campaign financing laws, with one MP labelling her "attack" style as harmful and dangerous.

image.altText: Joanna Howe stands next to women holding up ban feticide sign.

items.title: Serial harassers given '19 strikes' on Meta, eSafety boss says

children.content: Elon Musk's X Corp is fighting the eSafety Commissioner on three different take-down orders of violent content, including the killing of Charlie Kirk.

image.altText: A blonde woman in a teal suit.

items.title: Venezuela earthquake survivor pulled from rubble after eight days

children.content: A 43 year old man has been pulled from the rubble of a collapsed shopping centre after being trapped for eight days after earthquakes in Venezuela.

image.altText: a man on a stretcher with no shirt and an oxygen mask on

items.title: An 'incalculable' number of corpses are in Venezuela's earthquake zone. People feel abandoned

children.content: On the ground in Venezuela death and desperation linger, but they are juxtaposed against miracle survival stories.

articles.title: Micro 'cockroach' drones help find earthquake survivors in Venezuela

children.content: A week after devastating twin earthquakes struck Venezuela rescue teams are using drones, dogs, cameras and specialised listening equipment to search for survivors.

image.altText: A man walks among the rubble of a building that collapsed in earthquakes.

items.title: 'Night of horror' in Kyiv as at least 25 die in Russian attacks across Ukraine

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

image.altText: A building is damaged and on fire.

items.title: The ongoing cost of medical misogyny for women

children.content: Australia has come a long way when it comes to women's healthcare, but experts say medical misogyny is still very much alive — and it's not just attitudes that are the problem.

image.altText: A woman lies on the couch with one hand on her head and another on her lower abdomen

children.content: Spain takes on Austria on Los Angeles for a place in the World Cup round of 16. Later on, Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal faces Croatia and Switzerland does battle with Algeria. Follow live.

items.title: Spain on fire against Austria as Lamine Yamal dazzles
How World Cup watch parties in pubs and cafes took over Australia

image.altText: Man wearing yellow Ghana soccer jersey blows into a vuvuzela inside a party environment

children.content: They were just supposed to be somewhere to watch the match, but World Cup viewing parties have ended up becoming so much more.

items.title: Concussion awareness leaving parents 'terrified', experts say

children.content: A research paper published in June found almost a third of parents surveyed said they stopped their children from playing contact sports out of fear of concussion.

items.title: De Minaur cruises through to Wimbledon third round with straight-sets win

items.title: Childcare safety loophole for educators breaching regulations

children.content: A loophole in the government’s new childcare safety legislation allows educators who have breached safety standards to continue operating.

items.title: Former Olympian charged over alleged 'vandalism' of US Reflecting Pool

children.content: Former US Olympian David 'Davey' Hearn has been accused of "maliciously" breaking or destroying lining material on the bottom of the Washington DC pool, court documents show

items.title: Fuel security remains critical despite EV shift, Ampol boss says

items.title: Vatican excommunicates members of right-wing breakaway group SSPX

children.content: The Vatican has excommunicated priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval.

items.title: Victoria Park closure cuts international students' access to vital refuge

items.title: Husband welcomes drug-driving law change after crash that claimed wife

children.content: A man devastated by a drug-driving "loophole" that allowed a meth-affected driver to avoid a drug conviction after a fatal crash welcomes new legislation.

items.title: Patients at risk from 'toxic' mental health service, clinicians warn

children.content: Psychiatrist Dr Sarah Dahlenburg was so worried about her region's mental health service she warned her bosses. Two months later, a patient was dead.

items.title: UK PM apologises over forced adoptions, calls practice a 'stain on history'

children.content: UK PM Keir Starmer has formally apologised for the British state's role in separating tens of thousands of unmarried mothers from their babies.

image.altText: Keir Starmer sits in a room filled with women.

items.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart

items.title: Explosion in feral pig numbers attributed to major flooding in far north SA

children.content: Feral pig numbers are exploding in outback South Australia after record-breaking floods, prompting a call from station owners and the Invasive Species Council for governments to take action.

image.altText: A mob of feral pigs wading across and out of flood waters

items.title: Captains Flat residents welcome $1m to remove lead-contaminated soil

children.content: Residents of Captains Flat have welcomed the promise of a $1 million fund to help them remediate lead contamination on private properties, but some fear it will not be enough.

image.altText: Signs warning of dangerous lead contamination at the road to a closed mine.

items.title: State cracks down on sale of potentially deadly 'nangs'

children.content: After a 7.30 investigation exposed the increasing dangers of nitrous oxide use, NSW  has announced reforms will come into place shortly.
